contraventionDescription: Failing to comply with a sign indicating that vehiclular traffic must pass to the specified side of the sign. Contravention code 38

sections.heading: Failing to comply with a sign indicating that vehiclular traffic must pass to the specified side of the sign. Contravention code 38
sections.content: This contravention is now being enforced by local authorities. There are actually 4 suffixes to the contravention code as shown below.
sections.contentHtml: This contravention is now being enforced by local authorities. There are actually 4 suffixes to the contravention code as shown below.

sections.heading: Sample signs:
sections.content:
32d: proceeding in the wrong direction. This arrow can be pointing in any direction including straight ahead.
32p: Passing to the wrong side of the sign
32t: Turning in the wrong direction
32w: One way traffic
sections.contentHtml:
32d: proceeding in the wrong direction. This arrow can be pointing in any direction including straight ahead.

 

32p: Passing to the wrong side of the sign

 

32t: Turning in the wrong direction

 

32w: One way traffic

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
2 Check: What a Penalty Charge Notice must contain
3 Check: Statutory grounds for appeal for Moving Traffic Contraventions
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)

2 Check: What a Penalty Charge Notice must contain

3 Check: Statutory grounds for appeal for Moving Traffic Contraventions

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
Did the penalty charge notice that you received show the correct suffix. For example 32w relates to the sign showing the direction of traffic in a one way street. The signs above will confirm the correct suffix.
There are 8 more ways that you can have your penalty charge notice cancelled for this offence. We have even summarised adjudicator’s decisions where motorists have won.
Were the signs sufficiently clear, illuminated and on both sides of the road? If not the contravention is unenforceable.
Were the signs correct? In one case Transport for London (TFL) have been using the straight ahead arrow signs when really they should have been using a no right turn sign because the turning on the left was one way towards the road anyway. The adjudicator said it was confusing because the purpose of the straight ahead arrows was only really to stop traffic turning right. And so no right turn would have been the appropriate signs to install.
Remember that in order to enforce this restriction the enforcing authority must show you actually passing the signs with white arrows on a blue background. If their video evidence does not show this then they cannot enforce the restriction.
Were there road markings, if so did they contain exactly the same restrictions as the signs.
If there are restrictions at certain times were times stated on the Penalty charge notice (PCN) actually the same as on the signs.
The PCN must also show a picture of the sign which is essential as the offence is “failing to comply with a sign” etc. If the contravention description does not include “failing to comply with a sign… then the pcn does not have to show the sign but the council still has to show that clear and correct signage was present.
Did you commit the contravention because you had no alternative. In one case a lorry blocked the road so the motorist had no alterative. The appeal was allowed.
Also were there signs warning of CCTV camera enforcement in the vicinity. Whilst these are not compulsory under the traffic management act they are compulsory under the guidlines issued by the information commissioner’s office (ICO).
Don’t forget that you can cite compassionate ground as a basis for appealing a Penalty Charge Notice.
Now take a look at the adjudicators decisions for this contravention.
Back to Moving Traffic
